name: Michigan State University Extension - Ionia
description_short: Michigan State University Extension provides free, unbiased financial and homeownership education through certified counselors, webinars, and self-paced learning resources.
description_long:
Michigan State University Extension operates MI Money Health, a comprehensive educational program developed by a land-grant university to serve all Michigan residents regardless of financial status. The program is part of MSU's broader extension mission to provide trusted, research-backed education on personal finance and housing.

MI Money Health offers multiple formats of free education including online and in-person classes, one-on-one expert consultations with certified financial and housing counselors, self-paced articles and videos, webinars on specific topics like mortgage foreclosure basics and homeownership education, and community presentations. They cover money management fundamentals, homeownership education, foreclosure prevention and assistance, retirement planning, and emerging financial issues. Notable programs include the Rent Smart program on rental preparation and tenant conduct, and presentations on family wealth transfer and estate planning considerations.

What distinguishes MI Money Health is its delivery through an academic institution with certified housing and money management counselors providing unbiased education designed to be accessible and easy to understand. The program emphasizes reaching diverse audiences and removes barriers by offering both online and in-person options. They track and publish impact data on program outcomes for Michigan residents.

As a university extension program, MI Money Health is genuinely free with no hidden fees or sales agenda. However, it is education-focused rather than hands-on debt resolution—they counsel and educate rather than directly negotiate with creditors or dispute credit reports. Services are limited to Michigan residents, and access may vary by county or regional availability.
pros: Completely free education and counseling with no hidden fees or sales tactics
Certified housing and money management counselors providing unbiased guidance
Multiple learning formats including webinars, in-person classes, self-paced content, and one-on-one expert contact
Covers comprehensive topics from basic money management to foreclosure prevention and homeownership
Accessible to all Michigan residents regardless of financial status or background
University-backed program with published impact data and outcomes tracking
Specialized programs addressing emerging community issues like rental preparation and estate planning
cons: Limited to Michigan residents only—cannot serve out-of-state consumers
Education and counseling only; does not directly negotiate with creditors, dispute credit errors, or manage debt resolution
Regional availability may vary by county—access depends on local MSU Extension office resources
Relies on self-paced learning for some content, which requires self-motivation and may not suit all learners
No direct credit repair or debt settlement capabilities
best_for: Michigan homebuyers and renters seeking free, unbiased education on the buying/renting process
Homeowners facing potential foreclosure looking for prevention options and guidance
Families and individuals seeking foundational money management and financial planning education
Michigan residents wanting to build financial capability with certified, university-backed counseling
diagnosis: MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking free, unbiased financial and homeownership education from a trusted university source. The primary caveat is that this is an educational program, not a debt resolution or credit repair service—they counsel and inform rather than actively negotiate with creditors, dispute negative items, or directly resolve debt.
services: Free one-on-one consultations with certified financial counselors
Free one-on-one consultations with certified housing counselors
Mortgage foreclosure basics and prevention webinars
Money management and financial literacy classes
Self-paced learning articles, videos, and publications
Rent Smart program—rental preparation and tenant conduct guidance
Family wealth transfer and estate planning presentations
Youth financial education programs (Smart Money Missions)
Consumer protection and fraud awareness education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
